family_label: Schema, state, command, and event producer/consumer boundaries
notes: Generated from the governed beta compatibility report and skew-window register; do not widen row support in this manifest by hand.

downgrade_behavior.state_preservation_note: Additive-only downgrade preserves user-authored durable truth; meaning-changing downgrades refuse rather than rewrite state.

rows.notes: Descriptor and invocation-session consumers stay inside one command descriptor schema epoch and preserve unknown additive fields.

rollback_order.notes: Inline report window; rollback follows the row's downgrade behavior.

best_effort.combination_label: a consumer sees an additive ui_slot_hint it can ignore while still preserving the record
best_effort.notes: Render-only consumers may narrow their UI projection without claiming full parity on the new slot.

supported.combination_label: command_descriptor_schema_version and invocation payload contract stay in the same additive epoch
supported.notes: Unknown additive fields are preserved, and disabled-reason / preview / approval semantics retain their original meanings.

unsupported.combination_label: required field removed, repurposed, or interpreted differently across producers and consumers
unsupported.notes: Breaking command-shape drift blocks execution and claim publication; it is not a warning-only condition.

untested.combination_label: preview-channel surface lags a newly added additive field without updated fixtures
untested.notes: The seed reserves the case explicitly instead of treating it as silently supported.

skew_window.summary: Descriptor and invocation-session consumers stay inside one command descriptor schema epoch and preserve unknown additive fields. Required-field or meaning-changing drift is outside the claimed window.

unsupported_state_behavior.contract_rule: Consumers that cannot attribute a required field refuse the payload with an attributed error.

upgrade_order.notes: Inline report window; no wider rolling-upgrade order is claimed.

downgrade_behavior.state_preservation_note: Additive-only downgrade preserves user-authored durable truth. Meaning-changing downgrades refuse rather than rewrite state.

rows.notes: Profile, state-map, and restore-provenance records preserve one schema epoch with additive-only tolerance; meaning-changing downgrades refuse rather than rewrite state.

rollback_order.notes: Roll the producer back to the prior additive frontier before rolling the consumer back, so the consumer is never asked to parse a future-only field during rollback.

best_effort.combination_label: destination preserves layout and user-authored durable truth but cannot claim exact state parity
best_effort.notes: Layout-only and manual-review restores are explicit best-effort paths, not silent support.

supported.combination_label: source and destination preserve one profile schema epoch with additive-field tolerance
supported.notes: Supported restore may still truthfully downgrade from exact to compatible depending on validator outcomes.

unsupported.combination_label: excluded or admin-owned state is presented as portable user truth

untested.combination_label: machine-local addendum crosses a differently managed deployment profile

skew_window.summary: Producer and consumer preserve one schema epoch with additive-field tolerance; unknown additive fields are preserved, required-field or meaning-changing drift is out of window.

unsupported_state_behavior.contract_rule: Consumers that cannot attribute a required field refuse the payload with an attributed error. Silent truncation is forbidden. Explicitly excluded or admin-owned state rendered as portable user truth fails closed.

upgrade_order.notes: Producers may ship additive fields ahead of consumers provided consumers preserve unknown additive fields. Breaking epoch moves require consumer upgrades first.

downgrade_behavior.state_preservation_note: Audit continuity preserved across additive downgrades; no silent truncation.

rows.notes: Task-event producers and consumers preserve one stable epoch with additive-only field evolution; required-kind drift fails closed.

rollback_order.notes: Roll producers back to the prior additive frontier before rolling consumers back so retention and privacy class remain attributable.

best_effort.combination_label: heuristic parser producers emit canonical envelope fields with lower confidence
best_effort.notes: The envelope stays consumable, but the producer does not claim full source parity with native or structured adapters.

supported.combination_label: task-event producers and consumers preserve one stable epoch with additive-only field evolution

unsupported.combination_label: consumer cannot attribute a required payload kind or retention class

untested.combination_label: downstream consumer relies on appendix-defined envelope text before a dedicated schema export lands

skew_window.summary: Task-event and audit-event producers and consumers preserve one stable epoch with additive-only evolution. Consumers may lag producers within an epoch if unknown fields are preserved.

unsupported_state_behavior.contract_rule: Consumers that cannot attribute required payload kind or retention class refuse ingest with an attributed error. Silent truncation is forbidden.

upgrade_order.notes: Producers may ship additive fields ahead of consumers. Consumers that cannot preserve unknown additive fields must upgrade before the producer moves.
